items.title: Unauthenticated arbitrary file upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes (CVE-2026-53787) allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP object injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer leading to Remote Code Execution
items.summary: A critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer (CVE-2026-45247) allows remote attackers to achieve code execution by sending a crafted cookie. Patches were released on May 25, 2026, and CISA added it to its KEV catalog due to active exploitation.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This security update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, which could lead to arbitrary code execution, arbitrary file system write, application denial-of-service, and security feature bypass.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization (Auth Bypass) in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A critical incorrect authorization vulnerability (CVE-2026-34646) allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to bypass security checks and gain unauthorized write access to Adobe Commerce.

items.title: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption (DoS) in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A critical uncontrolled resource consumption flaw (CVE-2026-34651) in Adobe Commerce allows an unauthenticated attacker to exhaust system resources, leading to a denial-of-service condition.

items.title: Unauthenticated file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: A critical flaw, named PolyShell by Sansec, in Magento's REST API allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files by disguising code as an image, potentially leading to remote code execution. A fix was included in Magento 2.4.9, released on May 12, 2026.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: An incorrect authorization vulnerability (CVE-2026-34645) affecting Adobe Commerce was addressed as part of the APSB26-49 security update.

items.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability (CVE-2026-34647) affecting Adobe Commerce was addressed as part of the APSB26-49 security update.

items.title: Path Traversal in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A path traversal vulnerability (CVE-2026-34653) affecting Adobe Commerce was addressed as part of the APSB26-49 security update.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to security feature bypass, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read.
